prov.heading: Payment of certain allowances for long programme of study (other than secondary instruction)

history.history-note: Regulation 21: revoked, on 1 January 2013, by regulation 10 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2012 (SR 2012/212).

prov.heading: Payment of certain allowances for further programmes of study permitted for retraining for employment, etc

history.history-note: Regulation 22: revoked, on 1 January 2013, by regulation 10 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2012 (SR 2012/212).

prov.heading: Period for which certain allowances payable

para.text: A basic grant or independent circumstances grant must be paid for a period commencing with the beginning of the week during which, in the opinion of the chief executive, the portion of the recognised course of study in respect of which the allowance was awarded commences, and ending—
on the Sunday of the last week on which the last examination of the course in that year is conducted; or
at the end of the week during which, in the opinion of the chief executive, that portion ends, if that portion ends without any examination of the student.

Despite subclause (1), if the application for the basic grant or independent circumstances grant was received more than 7 days after the commencement of the portion of the recognised course of study (being a portion that commences on or after 1 January 2005), a basic grant or independent circumstances grant must not be paid for any period before the beginning of the week in which the application was received unless the application has been brought within any extended time for the application to be made given under regulation 40(7).
The period for which a basic grant or independent circumstances grant to which subclause (1A) applies must be paid commences with—
the beginning of the week in which the application for it is received; or
if the application for it is brought within any extended time allowed under regulation 40(7), on the same day on which payment would have commenced under subclause (1).

Despite subclause (1), the chief executive may extend or reduce by such number of weeks as the chief executive thinks fit the period in any year for which payments under a basic grant or an independent circumstances grant are made, in any particular case or class or classes of case, to provide for any variation in the course prescribed by the principal of any secondary school or the governing body of any tertiary provider.

history.history-note: Regulation 23(1A): inserted, on 7 October 2004, by regulation 7 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2004 (SR 2004/299).
Regulation 23(1B): inserted, on 7 October 2004, by regulation 7 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2004 (SR 2004/299).

prov.label: 23A
prov.heading: Limitation on payment for permanent residents enrolled at New Zealand campus studying overseas

para.text: This regulation applies to a student who is—
a permanent resident; and
eligible to receive an allowance continued under regulation 3(a) to (c); and
undertaking a tertiary course of study part of which is or will be done—
by the student overseas; and
while the student is enrolled at a New Zealand campus or delivery site of a tertiary provider.
Payments may be made to the student while the student is studying overseas only if—
the student is enrolled as an intramural student; and
the overseas study is or will be done in a country with which the student is not familiar.
For the purposes of subclause (2)﻿(b), a student is not familiar with a country if the student—
has not previously been to that country; or
has, in the 5 years before the date on which the overseas study begins, spent a total of less than 6 months in that country.

history.history-note: Regulation 23A: inserted, on 27 October 2016, by regulation 7 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016 (LI 2016/217).

prov.label: 23B
prov.heading: Limitation on payment for students enrolled at overseas campus or delivery site of tertiary provider

para.text: This regulation applies to a student who is—
eligible to receive an allowance continued under regulation 3(a) to (c); and
undertaking a course of study part of which is or will be done—
by the student overseas; and
while the student is enrolled at an overseas campus or delivery site of a tertiary provider.
Payments may be made to the student while the student is studying overseas only if—
the student is enrolled as an intramural student; and
the course of study is a component of a qualification listed at level 7 on the Qualifications and Credentials Framework, or a component of a Bachelors Honours degree; and
the study is or will be done in a specified country; and
in the case of a permanent resident, the study is or will be done in a country with which the student is not familiar.
For the purposes of subclause (2)﻿(d), a student is not familiar with a country if the student—
has not previously been to that country; or
has, in the 5 years before the date on which the overseas study begins, spent a total of less than 6 months in that country.

history.history-note: Regulation 23B: inserted, on 27 October 2016, by regulation 7 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016 (LI 2016/217).
Regulation 23B(2)﻿(b): amended, on 1 August 2022, by section 74 of the Education and Training Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 38).

prov.heading: Amount of accommodation benefit

para.text: The amount of an accommodation benefit per week is the amount, not exceeding the applicable weekly value of the benefit specified in Schedule 3, that may be determined in any case in accordance with guidelines issued from time to time by the chief executive.
Subclause (1) applies unless regulation 24A applies.
Any guidelines issued under subclause (1) may make provision for the payment of different rates in different regions, and for different types of accommodation.

Before issuing any guidelines under subclause (1), the chief executive must consider such information as is reasonably available to the chief executive about accommodation costs in different regions and for different types of accommodation.

history.history-note: Regulation 24 heading: amended, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 8(1) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).
Regulation 24(1): amended, on 1 October 2002, by regulation 6 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2002 (SR 2002/257).
Regulation 24(1A): inserted, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 8(2) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).
Regulation 24(4): revoked, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 8(3) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).
Regulation 24(5): revoked, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 8(3) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).

prov.label: 24A
prov.heading: Amount of accommodation benefit for single student with supported child

para.text: This regulation applies to a single student who—
has 1 or more supported children; and
is eligible for an accommodation benefit in respect of a portion of a recognised course of study commencing on or after 1 July 2015.
The amount of the accommodation benefit per week is the appropriate rate of accommodation supplement that the student would receive if the student were a beneficiary who—
received sole parent support under the Social Security Act 2018; and
was entitled to receive, and had been granted, an accommodation supplement.
For the purposes of subclause (2), the appropriate rate of accommodation supplement must be determined as provided in subclauses (3A) and (3B).

If, under section 65(1)﻿(b) of the Social Security Act 2018, the student does not meet the assets requirement (as set out in regulations made under section 423 of that Act), the appropriate rate of accommodation supplement is zero.

If, under section 65(1)﻿(b) of the Social Security Act 2018, the student meets the assets requirement (as set out in regulations made under section 423 of that Act), the appropriate rate of accommodation supplement must be determined in accordance with the following legislation:
section 17 and Part 7 of Schedule 4 of that Act; and
regulations made under section 423 of that Act.
In this regulation, beneficiary has the meaning given in Schedule 2 of the Social Security Act 2018.

history.history-note: Regulation 24A: inserted, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 9 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).
Regulation 24A(2)﻿(a): amended, on 26 November 2018, by section 459 of the Social Security Act 2018 (2018 No 32).
Regulation 24A(3): replaced, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).
Regulation 24A(3A): inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).
Regulation 24A(3B): inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).
Regulation 24A(4): amended, on 26 November 2018, by section 459 of the Social Security Act 2018 (2018 No 32).

prov.label: 24B
prov.heading: Period for which accommodation benefit payable

para.text: Payments under an accommodation benefit must be made for a period commencing not earlier than the date the chief executive specifies, which date must not precede the date payments are made under the student's basic grant or independent circumstances grant.

An amount of accommodation benefit is not payable to a student for a week for which the value of the basic grant or independent circumstances grant payable to the student is reduced to nil because of the student's personal income for the week.

history.history-note: Regulation 24B: inserted, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 9 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).

prov.heading: Payment of A-Bursaries and B-Bursaries

history.history-note: Regulation 25: revoked, on 15 December 2006 (applying in respect of allowances payable on and after 1 January 2007), by regulation 6(3) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2006 (SR 2006/356).

prov.heading: Payments may be made where work undertaken outside New Zealand

para.text: Where the chief executive is satisfied that it is desirable for the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) to undertake any work outside New Zealand, the chief executive may declare specified work intended to be done by that student outside New Zealand to be a full-time course in respect of that student only; and payments may be made to that student while he or she is carrying out that work outside New Zealand.

Subclause (3) applies where the chief executive is satisfied that it is necessary or desirable for the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) to undertake a short course of study overseas to augment—
a full-time course that that recipient is undertaking in New Zealand as part of a programme; or
a full-time course that—
does not constitute the completion of the programme of which it forms part; but
has been satisfactorily completed by the recipient in New Zealand.

Where subclause (2) applies, the chief executive of the Ministry of Education may, for the purposes of this subclause, declare that course to be associated with that programme and that recipient may be paid an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) to the same extent as would be appropriate if that course, and all travel reasonably necessary to enable that recipient to undertake that course, were part of that programme and being undertaken at the tertiary provider at which the recipient is enrolled in that programme.

prov.heading: No payment if income limit exceeded

history.history-note: Regulation 27: revoked, on 1 September 2005, by regulation 13 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2005 (SR 2005/254).

prov.heading: Suspension of allowances during detention in prison

para.text: A student who is imprisoned or detained in a prison, as defined in section 3(1) of the Corrections Act 2004, pursuant to a sentence of imprisonment or preventive detention is not entitled to receive any payment under these regulations in respect of any period of any such imprisonment or detention.

history.history-note: Regulation 28 heading: amended, on 1 June 2005, by section 207 of the Corrections Act 2004 (2004 No 50).
Regulation 28(1): amended, on 27 October 2016, by regulation 8 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016 (LI 2016/217).
Regulation 28(1): amended, on 1 June 2005, by section 207 of the Corrections Act 2004 (2004 No 50).

Regulation 28(2): revoked, on 15 December 2006 (applying in respect of allowances payable on and after 1 January 2007), by regulation 6(3) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2006 (SR 2006/356).

prov.heading: Suspension of certain allowances for failure to attend or perform work

para.text: Subject to regulation 17, if any student—
fails to attend regularly for classes appropriate to his or her course of study; or
fails to submit the work required by the teachers or lecturers of that course or any part of it,—
the chief executive may order the suspension of any allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) which is received by the student for that course.

Where the chief executive orders the suspension of any allowance under this regulation, the student ceases to be entitled to that allowance, and it must be suspended at the end of the week during which the chief executive so orders.

Where an allowance is suspended under this regulation, the chief executive may reinstate that allowance in the year of its suspension if, in that year, the recipient resumes meeting the requirements of the course of study which he or she is undertaking.

prov.heading: Suspension of certain allowances for failure of full-time course of study (other than secondary instruction)

para.text: Subject to regulations 12A and 17 and this regulation, an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) must be suspended if the amount of work (if any) passed by the recipient in the most recent course of study for which an allowance was paid (the relevant course) was, in the opinion of the chief executive, equivalent to half of or less than half of a full-time course.
Where—

the chief executive is satisfied that the failure of the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) to pass the required proportion of a full-time course is due to reasons beyond the recipient's control; or
the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) awarded in respect of a Masters degree has not completed the degree due to reasons beyond the recipient's control,—

the chief executive may direct that that allowance should not be suspended despite the amount of work the recipient has passed in the relevant course; and, in that case, that allowance may not be suspended merely because of the work passed by that recipient in the relevant course.

Where the chief executive gives a direction under subclause (2), the chief executive may also direct that the recipient of the allowance concerned may receive payments under that allowance for such further period as the chief executive specifies which may exceed the maximum period of eligibility for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c), allowed under regulation 20(1)﻿(b).
Nothing in this regulation applies to secondary students.

history.history-note: Regulation 30 heading: amended, on 1 October 2010, by regulation 11(1) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2010 (SR 2010/291).
Regulation 30(1): amended, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 10 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).
Regulation 30(1): amended, on 19 January 2007, by regulation 6(1)﻿(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 4) 2006 (SR 2006/379).
Regulation 30(1): amended, on 19 January 2007, by regulation 6(1)﻿(b) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 4) 2006 (SR 2006/379).
Regulation 30(2): amended, on 19 January 2007, by regulation 6(2) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 4) 2006 (SR 2006/379).
Regulation 30(3): amended, on 1 October 2010, by regulation 11(2) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2010 (SR 2010/291).

prov.heading: Suspension of certain allowances for failure to give statement

para.text: If a student awarded an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) does not give the chief executive a statement in accordance with regulation 16, the chief executive must suspend the allowance.

prov.heading: Reinstatement of certain allowances

para.text: A student whose allowance has been suspended under regulation 30 is entitled to have it reinstated if the student satisfies the chief executive that he or she has, since the suspension, passed work that is or forms part of a course of study that is, in the opinion of the chief executive, equivalent to more than half of the work of a full-time course.

A student whose allowance has been suspended under regulation 31 will be entitled to have it reinstated if the student satisfies the chief executive that he or she has, since the suspension, rectified the failure to give a statement in accordance with regulation 16.
Nothing in subclause (1) applies to secondary students.
Nothing in subclause (1) applies to any allowance continued under regulation 3(d) or (e).

prov.heading: Postponement of course of study

para.text: Where—
the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) has, with the consent of the chief executive, postponed his or her course of study; and

before postponing that course he or she had successfully completed work that is, in the opinion of the chief executive, equivalent to at least more than half of that part of the work of the course of study that was offered before that postponement and in respect of which the allowance concerned was awarded; and
that recipient resumes the course of study concerned at approximately the same point at a subsequent time and at a similar point in the course at which it was postponed,—

then, only the weeks that the student was attending the course count as part of the maximum period of eligibility for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c), allowed under regulation 20(1)﻿(a) or, if applicable, 20(1)﻿(b).
Subclause (1) is subject to regulation 47A(f) (which relates to an application after 30 September 2010 for an allowance for a course commencing before 1 January 2011).

Nothing in subclause (1) entitles the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) which is awarded in respect of a course of study postponed under that subclause to be paid that allowance for any period between the postponement of that course and its resumption.

history.history-note: Regulation 33(1): amended, on 1 October 2010, by regulation 12(1) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2010 (SR 2010/291).
Regulation 33(1A): inserted, on 1 October 2010, by regulation 12(2) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2010 (SR 2010/291).

prov.heading: Suspension of certain allowances due to tertiary provider losing accreditation

para.text: If a tertiary provider loses its accreditation or course approval under the Act, allowances continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) which are received by students undertaking approved courses at that tertiary provider are suspended.

Any allowance suspended under subclause (1) will be reinstated if that accreditation or course approval is granted again before the end of the same academic year or before such time as the chief executive may determine.
Despite subclause (1), the chief executive may, at his or her discretion, decide that a student should not lose an allowance under this regulation.

prov.heading: Suspension of certain allowances due to injury, sickness, or holidays

para.text: Where a student does not attend a secondary school or tertiary provider for a period exceeding 2 weeks because of injury or sickness, the chief executive may suspend the payment of any allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) until that student resumes meeting the requirements of the course of study which he or she is undertaking.

Where a student who is not on study leave does not attend any course conducted at a secondary school or tertiary provider for a period of more than 3 weeks, because of a break in tuition at the secondary school or tertiary institution, the payment of any allowance continued under regulation 3(a) to (c) must be suspended until the staff of the secondary school or tertiary institution resume teaching a course in which the student is enrolled, unless the chief executive permits payment to continue.

prov.heading: Lodging of appeals

para.text: Every appeal must be by notice in writing lodged with the Authority within 21 days after the decision appealed against, or such longer period as the Authority in any case allows.
Every such notice must—
be signed by the appellant or a person authorised by the appellant to act on his or her behalf; and
specify the decision appealed against; and
provide an address for the sending of communications in connection with the appeal.

prov.heading: Relevant chief executive or Minister of Education to be given copies of notices of appeal

para.text: Immediately after the lodgement of a notice of appeal, the Authority must send a copy of that notice to the chief executive of the department of State who made the decision appealed against, or to the Minister of Education, if that Minister made the decision appealed against.
As soon as possible after receiving a copy of any notice of appeal, the relevant chief executive, or the Minister of Education, as the case may require, must send to the Authority—

all applications, documents, written submissions, statements, reports, and other papers relating to the decision appealed against, that are in the possession or under the control of the relevant chief executive, or the Minister of Education; and
a copy of the decision appealed against; and
a report setting out the matters to which the relevant chief executive or the Minister of Education had regard in making the decision appealed against; and
a statement of any other matters that the relevant chief executive or the Minister of Education wishes to draw to the attention of the Authority.
Immediately after receiving a report under subclause (2)﻿(c), the Authority must send a copy of it, and of any statement under subclause (2)﻿(d), to the appellant concerned.
An appellant may send to the Authority—
any comments the appellant has on the report, and on any statement, a copy of which has been sent to the appellant under subclause (3); or
notice that the appellant wishes to discontinue the appeal; and, in that case, the appeal will be discontinued.
The Authority must not determine any appeal until the expiration of 14 days after the date on which a copy of the report under subclause (2)﻿(c) was sent to the appellant.
Where, before an appeal is decided, the Authority receives from the appellant comments sent to the Authority under subclause (4)﻿(a),—

the Authority must immediately send a copy to the relevant chief executive or to the Minister of Education, as the case may require, who, as soon as possible, must send to the Authority either any comments he or she wishes to make on those comments or a new decision; and

the Authority must not determine the appeal concerned until the expiration of 14 days after the date on which the copy was sent to the relevant chief executive or to the Minister of Education, as the case may require.
Where, under subclause (6)﻿(a), the relevant chief executive or the Minister of Education sends the Authority a new decision,—
he or she must also send a copy to the appellant; and
without affecting the appellant's right to appeal against the new decision, the appeal against the old decision is considered to have been discontinued.

prov.heading: Evidence

para.text: The Authority has full discretionary power to request further written evidence from any appellant on questions of fact, and may require any such evidence to be verified by statutory declaration.

In the exercise of its powers the Authority may receive as evidence any statement, document, information, or matter, that, in its opinion, may assist it to deal with any matter before it, whether or not the same would be admissible in a court of law.

prov.heading: Matters to which Authority to have regard

para.text: In reaching any decision, the Authority must have regard to—
the notice of appeal concerned; and
all documents sent to the authority under regulation 37(2) or (4) or (6); and
all evidence received by it—
whether or not that notice, those documents, or that evidence, or any part of it or them, would be admissible in a court of law.
On the determination of an appeal, the Authority must notify the chief executive and the appellant, in writing, of the Authority's decision and the reasons for it.

prov.heading: Applications

para.text: Any person who, in respect of any year, is enrolled or proposes to apply for enrolment in a recognised course of study at a secondary school or a tertiary provider may apply to the chief executive, on a form approved by the chief executive for the purpose, for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) to be awarded to him or her in respect of that course.
Every applicant for an allowance must forward the application to the chief executive.

The chief executive may require any applicant for the award of an allowance, before being awarded that allowance, to make a statement or to provide information in some other form as to any matters that, under these regulations, are conditions precedent to the award of that allowance to that applicant.

Where a statement or information has been required under subclause (3), unless the chief executive is satisfied that there are special circumstances justifying a delay in the provision of that statement or information beyond the applicable date specified in paragraph (a) or paragraph (b), the applicant must provide that statement or information to the chief executive—
on or before 31 December, in the year in which the application is made; or
if the portion of the recognised course of study for which the student applied for the allowance commences on or after 1 January 2005, before that portion of the course ends.
Where a statement or information or evidence has been required under subclause (3) or regulation 4(2)﻿(b), the applicant—
must not be awarded that allowance until he or she has made that statement or has provided that information or supplied that evidence; and

must not be awarded that allowance if he or she has made that statement or has provided that information or supplied that evidence, as the case may require, after the date by which the applicant was required to provide it.

Except in the circumstances described in subclause (7), where a student would, but for his or her omission to make an application, become entitled to receive an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) during his or her course of study, his or her application may be considered only if it is made within 60 days of the day on which he or she would become so entitled.
Subclause (6) applies only in respect of an application made by a student in respect of a portion of a course of study that commences before 1 January 2005.

The chief executive may extend the time allowed for applying for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (e) if he or she is satisfied that in the particular case there are special circumstances warranting an extension of time.

history.history-note: Regulation 40(1): amended, on 1 September 2013, by regulation 7 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 (SR 2013/324).
Regulation 40(4): substituted, on 7 October 2004, by regulation 9(1) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2004 (SR 2004/299).
Regulation 40(6A): inserted, on 7 October 2004, by regulation 9(2) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2004 (SR 2004/299).

prov.heading: Notification of change in student's circumstances affecting entitlement

para.text: If there occurs in the circumstances of the recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) any change of such a nature that it affects or may affect the recipient's entitlement to that allowance, the rate at which it is paid, or payment of it, he or she must, without delay, give notification of that change to the chief executive.
The recipient of an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) must notify the chief executive of—
any change of residence by the recipient; and
any commencement of residence by the recipient with the recipient's parents.

prov.heading: Form of statement for continuing eligibility for certain allowances

para.text: The chief executive may specify from time to time the form of the statement required to be given under regulation 16 in order to maintain continuing eligibility for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c).

The form of statement specified under subclause (1) may include any information considered by the chief executive to be relevant to determining the recipient's entitlement to receive an allowance continued under regulation 3(a) to (c), the rate at which it is paid, or the payment of it.

prov.label: 42A
prov.heading: Relationship status for student allowance purposes

para.text: For the purposes of determining any application for any allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c), or of reviewing any allowance already granted, or of determining the rate of any allowance, the chief executive—
may regard as single any applicant or student who is married or in a civil union but is—
estranged from his or her spouse or civil union partner; and
lives apart from that person; and
may determine a date on which they are to be regarded as having commenced to live apart; and
may then, in the chief executive's discretion, grant an allowance, refuse to grant an allowance, or terminate, reduce, or increase any allowance already granted, from that date accordingly.

history.history-note: Regulation 42A: inserted, on 15 December 2006 (applying in respect of allowances payable on and after 1 January 2007), by regulation 7 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2006 (SR 2006/356).

prov.heading: Calculation of personal income

para.text: Where the personal income or combined income of a person for a week is required to be calculated for the purposes of these regulations, and the amount for the week of the personal income or, as the case requires, the combined income cannot be readily ascertained (for example, because the person or spouse or partner is self-employed), that income may be calculated by the chief executive by—

dividing the person's total personal or combined income, as the case may require, over a period of such number of weeks specified by the chief executive (not exceeding 52) by the number of weeks in that period; and
deducting from the figure derived from paragraph (a) such items, if any, that the chief executive is satisfied are likely to reduce that income, or combined income, as the case may require; and
adding to the figure derived from paragraph (a), such items, if any, that the chief executive is satisfied are likely to increase that income, or combined income, as the case may require.

history.history-note: Regulation 43: amended, on 1 September 2005, by regulation 14(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2005 (SR 2005/254).
Regulation 43: amended, on 1 September 2005, by regulation 14(b) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 3) 2005 (SR 2005/254).
Regulation 43: amended, on 26 April 2005, by section 12 of the Relationships (Statutory References) Act 2005 (2005 No 3).
Regulation 43: amended, on 7 October 2004, by regulation 10 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2004 (SR 2004/299).

prov.heading: Deprivation of income

para.text: Despite any thing to the contrary in these regulations, the chief executive may, in any particular case, in respect of any allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c),—
terminate the allowance; or
reduce the allowance; or
grant the allowance at a reduced rate—

if the chief executive is satisfied that the applicant or any parent, spouse, or partner of the applicant has directly or indirectly deprived himself or herself of any income resulting in the applicant becoming eligible for that allowance or payment of that allowance at an increased rate.
In this regulation, income means,—
in the case of an applicant, personal income:
in the case of the spouse or partner of an applicant, spousal or partner's income:
in the case of a parent of an applicant, parental income.

history.history-note: Regulation 44(1): amended, on 1 October 2011 (applying to allowances for courses of study that commence on or after 1 January 2012 (whether the allowance was first payable to students before, on, or after that date)), by regulation 7(1) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2011 (SR 2011/288).

Regulation 44(1): amended, on 1 October 2011 (applying to allowances for courses of study that commence on or after 1 January 2012 (whether the allowance was first payable to students before, on, or after that date)), by regulation 7(2) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2011 (SR 2011/288).

Regulation 44(2): added, on 1 October 2011 (applying to allowances for courses of study that commence on or after 1 January 2012 (whether the allowance was first payable to students before, on, or after that date)), by regulation 7(3) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2011 (SR 2011/288).

prov.heading: Review of certain allowances

para.text: The chief executive may from time to time review any allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) in order to ascertain—
whether the recipient of the allowance is entitled to receive it; or
whether the recipient of the allowance may not be, or may not have been entitled to receive that allowance, or the rate of allowance that is or was payable to the recipient of the allowance.

If, after reviewing an allowance, the chief executive is satisfied that the recipient of the allowance is no longer or was not entitled to receive the allowance or is or was entitled to receive it at a different rate, the chief executive may suspend, terminate, or vary the rate of that allowance from such date as the chief executive reasonably determines.

If, after reviewing an allowance, the chief executive considers that the recipient of the allowance is more appropriately entitled to receive some other allowance, the chief executive may, in his or her discretion, cancel the allowance and grant the recipient of that allowance another type of allowance, commencing from the date of cancellation.

prov.heading: Certain allowances to be inalienable

para.text: Except as provided in any other enactment, a student who receives an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) may not—
assign the allowance:
permit the allowance to be made the subject of a charge.
Where a student contravenes subclause (1), the chief executive may, in respect of any allowance continued under regulation 3(a) to (c) that is received by the student,—
terminate the allowance; or
reduce the allowance.

prov.heading: Transitional provision

history.history-note: Regulation 47: revoked, on 27 October 2016, by regulation 9 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016 (LI 2016/217).

prov.label: 47A
prov.heading: Applications on or after 1 October 2010 for allowances for courses commencing before 1 January 2011

history.history-note: Regulation 47A: revoked, on 1 July 2015, by regulation 11 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2015 (LI 2015/135).

prov.label: 47B
prov.heading: Transitional provisions: 1 September 2013 changes to residence-related eligibility periods

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: General: only 2014 and later courses affected

para.text: An application is subject to regulation 12(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii) (as amended on 1 September 2013 by regulation 5(1) of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013) only if the application is for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) in respect of a course of study commencing on or after 1 January 2014.

An application made on or after 1 September 2013 for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) in respect of a course of study commencing before 1 January 2014 is therefore subject to regulation 12(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii) (as in force immediately before 1 September 2013).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception: compliance in 2013 with former requirements, and studying in or after 2014

para.text: An application must be treated as if the applicant complies with regulation 12(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii) (as amended on 1 September 2013 by regulation 5(1) of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013) if—
the application is for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) in respect of a course of study commencing on or after 1 January 2014; and

the chief executive is satisfied that the applicant, at any time or times before the close of 31 December 2014, complied with the requirements of regulation 12(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii) (as in force immediately before 1 September 2013).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception: when allowance reliant on it commences

para.text: No allowance granted in reliance on subclause (3) can commence before the date on which the applicant first complies with the requirements of regulation 12(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii) (as in force immediately before 1 September 2013).

history.history-note: Regulation 47B: inserted, on 1 September 2013, by regulation 8 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 (SR 2013/324).

prov.label: 47C
prov.heading: Transitional provisions: exception to ineligibility ground introduced on 1 September 2013

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Allowance application approved for study in 2013, and studying in 2014

para.text: An application is not subject to regulation 12(1)﻿(ab) (as inserted on 1 September 2013 by regulation 5(3) of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013) if—
the application is for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) in respect of a course of study commencing on or after 1 January 2014; and
the chief executive is satisfied that the applicant,—
is or will be, when that course of study commences, of or over the age specified in section 7(1) of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001; and
either—

before the close of 31 December 2013 had approved by the chief executive an application for (whether or not the applicant received) an allowance of that kind for any earlier course or courses of study commencing on or after 1 January 2013; or
on or after 1 January 2013 and before the close of 31 December 2013 received an allowance of that kind for any earlier course or courses of study commencing on or after 1 January 2012.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception ends with close of 31 December 2014

para.text: The exception in subclause (1) ends at, and so is unavailable to the applicant after, the close of 31 December 2014.

history.history-note: Regulation 47C: inserted, on 1 September 2013, by regulation 8 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 (SR 2013/324).

prov.label: 47D
prov.heading: Transitional provisions: exception to 120-week maximum entitlement period introduced on 1 September 2013

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Allowance application approved for study in 2013, and studying in 2014

para.text: An application is not subject to regulation 20(1)﻿(b) and (3) (as amended on 1 September 2013 by regulation 6 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013), and is instead subject to the 200-week maximum entitlement under regulation 20(1)﻿(b) and (3) (as in force immediately before 1 September 2013), if—
the application is for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c) in respect of a course of study commencing on or after 1 January 2014; and
the chief executive is satisfied that the applicant,—
is or will be, when that course of study commences, of or over the age of 40 years; and
either—

before the close of 31 December 2013 had approved by the chief executive an application for (whether or not the applicant received) an allowance of that kind for any earlier course or courses of study commencing on or after 1 January 2013; or
on or after 1 January 2013 and before the close of 31 December 2013 received an allowance of that kind for any earlier course or courses of study commencing on or after 1 January 2012.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exception ends with close of 31 December 2014

para.text: The exception in subclause (1) ends at, and so is unavailable to the applicant after, the close of 31 December 2014.

history.history-note: Regulation 47D: inserted, on 1 September 2013, by regulation 8 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 (SR 2013/324).

prov.label: 47E
prov.heading: Savings provisions: extended eligibility granted, or sought but not determined, on 1 September 2013

para.text: Extended eligibility sought and granted under regulation 20(7) before 1 September 2013 continues on and after that date as if the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 had not been made.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Extended eligibility sought but not determined

para.text: Extended eligibility sought under regulation 20(7), but not determined (declined or granted), before 1 September 2013 must be determined on or after that date as if the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 had not been made.

history.history-note: Regulation 47E: inserted, on 1 September 2013, by regulation 8 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2013 Initiatives) Amendment Regulations 2013 (SR 2013/324).

prov.heading: Previous allowances

para.text: Where a student has been paid a basic grant, independent circumstances grant, or accommodation benefit in any year before 1 January 1999, the following provisions apply for the purpose of calculating the continuing entitlement (if any) of that student for an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c):
any course of 1 academic year (37 weeks or more) must be treated as 40 weeks:
any course of less than 1 academic year (12 weeks to 36 weeks) must be treated as the number of actual weeks the student was paid an allowance.

Despite subclause (1), where a student has been paid a basic grant, independent circumstances grant, or accommodation benefit in any year before 1 January 1999, solely in respect of a course of study undertaken at a secondary school, the period during which the student was paid an allowance must be disregarded for the purposes of determining the continuing entitlement (if any) of that student to an allowance continued by regulation 3(a) to (c).

prov.heading: Revocations

para.text: The regulations specified in Schedule 4 are revoked.

history.history-note: Schedule 1: replaced, on 27 October 2016, by regulation 10 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016 (LI 2016/217).

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2017

para.text: This clause applies to the amendments made to these regulations by regulations 4, 5, 7, 10, and 11 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016.
Those amendments apply only to overseas study starting on or after 1 January 2017.
Subclause (2) applies even if the enrolment concerned was applied for, or permitted, or both, before 1 January 2017.

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances (Increase for 2018) Amendment Regulations 2017

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 2: inserted, on 1 January 2018, by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances (Increase for 2018) Amendment Regulations 2017 (LI 2017/294).

prov.heading: Amendments apply to courses of study commencing before, on, or after 1 January 2018

para.text: This clause applies to the rates in Schedule 2 as amended by regulation 5(2) of the Student Allowances (Increase for 2018) Amendment Regulations 2017.
Those rates must be used for any payment on or after 1 January 2018 of an allowance—
continued by regulation 3(a) (basic grant) or regulation 3(b) (independent circumstances grant); and
granted in respect of a course of study commencing before, on, or after 1 January 2018.

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2018

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 3: inserted, on 1 January 2019, by regulation 5 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2018 (LI 2018/178).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2019

para.text: This clause applies to the amendments to these regulations made by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2018.
The amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2019.
Subclause (2) applies even if the enrolment concerned was applied for or permitted, or both, before 1 January 2019.

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances (State-supported Care Exemptions) Amendment Regulations 2019

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 4: inserted, on 13 December 2019, by regulation 5 of the Student Allowances (State-supported Care Exemptions) Amendment Regulations 2019 (LI 2019/307).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2020

para.text: This clause applies to the amendments to these regulations made by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances (State-supported Care Exemptions) Amendment Regulations 2019.
The amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2020.
Subclause (2) applies even if the enrolment concerned was applied for or permitted, or both, before 1 January 2020.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 4: inserted, on 13 December 2019, by regulation 5 of the Student Allowances (State-supported Care Exemptions) Amendment Regulations 2019 (LI 2019/307).

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances (Christchurch Mosques Attack) Amendment Regulations 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 5: inserted, on 21 September 2020, by regulation 6 of the Student Allowances (Christchurch Mosques Attack) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/168).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2021

para.text: This clause applies to the amendments to these regulations made by the Student Allowances (Christchurch Mosques Attack) Amendment Regulations 2020.
The amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2021.
Subclause (2) applies even if the enrolment concerned was applied for or permitted, or both, before 1 January 2021.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 5: inserted, on 21 September 2020, by regulation 6 of the Student Allowances (Christchurch Mosques Attack) Amendment Regulations 2020 (LI 2020/168).

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2022

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 6: inserted, on 27 March 2022, by regulation 6(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2022 (SL 2022/66).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 May 2022

para.text: This clause applies to the amendments to these regulations made by the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2022.
The amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 May 2022.
Subclause (2) applies even if the enrolment concerned was applied for or permitted, or both, before 1 May 2022.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 6: inserted, on 27 March 2022, by regulation 6(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2022 (SL 2022/66).

part.heading: Provision relating to Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2023

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 7: inserted, on 16 October 2023, by regulation 5(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2023 (SL 2023/176).

prov.heading: Amendments apply only to study starting on or after 1 January 2024

para.text: This clause applies to the amendments made to these regulations by regulation 4(2) and (3) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2023.
The amendments apply only to a course of study starting on or after 1 January 2024.
Subclause (2) applies even if enrolment for the course of study was applied for or permitted, or both, before 1 January 2024.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 7: inserted, on 16 October 2023, by regulation 5(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2023 (SL 2023/176).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 8: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 5(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).

prov.heading: Interpretation

para.text: In this Part, amendment regulations means the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 8: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 5(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).

prov.heading: How Ministry must decide accommodation benefit application made, but not decided, before 2 March 2026

para.text: This clause applies if, before 2 March 2026,—
an application for an accommodation benefit was made; and
the application—
was not withdrawn; and
did not lapse; and
the Ministry did not make a decision in relation to the application.
The amendment made to regulation 24A by the amendment regulations does not apply for the purposes of the Ministry deciding the application.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 9: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 5(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).

prov.heading: Transitional provision for persons granted accommodation benefit before 2 March 2026

para.text: This clause applies to a person who—
was receiving an accommodation benefit under these regulations immediately before 2 March 2026; or
was granted an accommodation benefit under these regulations before 2 March 2026 that—
commences on or after 2 March 2026; or
immediately before 2 March 2026, was subject to a suspension.
The amendment made to regulation 24A by the amendment regulations applies to the person for weeks starting on or after 2 March 2026.
This clause is subject to clause 9.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 10: inserted, on 2 March 2026, by regulation 5(a) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2025 (SL 2025/307).
Schedule 2: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).
Schedule 2 Part 1: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).
Schedule 2 Subpart 1: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).

prov.heading: Combined income not more than $569.40 for week

para.text: The following table sets out the value of a basic grant payable for a week to a married or partnered student whose combined income for the week is not more than $569.40:

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. Column 1 is headed Item. Column 2 is headed Circumstances. Column 3 is headed Value ($).

row.entry: The student’s spouse or partner is not enrolled in more than half of a full-time course, and—
the student has 1 or more supported children
The student’s spouse or partner is enrolled in more than half of a full-time course but does not receive, and is not eligible for, a grant, and—
the student has 1 or more supported children
The student’s spouse or partner is enrolled in more than half of a full-time course but does not receive, although is eligible for, a grant, and—
the student has 1 or more supported children
The student’s spouse or partner is enrolled in more than half of a full-time course and receives a grant, and—
the student has 1 or more supported children

para.text: However, if the student’s personal income exceeds $284.70 for the week, the value of the basic grant for the week is reduced by an amount equalling the excess.

history.history-note: Schedule 2 clause 1: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).

prov.heading: Combined income between $569.40 and $1,185.75 for week

para.text: The following table sets out the value of a basic grant payable for a week to a married or partnered student whose combined income for the week is more than $569.40:

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. Column 1 is headed Item. Column 2 is headed Circumstances. Column 3 is headed Value ($).

row.entry: is living in the same home as their spouse or partner:
is not living in the same home as their spouse or partner
The student has 1 or more supported children and is living in the same home as their spouse or partner, and—
the student’s spouse or partner is not enrolled in more than half of a full-time course:
the student’s spouse or partner is enrolled in more than half of a full-time course and—
is eligible for but does not receive, or is not eligible for, a grant
The student has 1 or more supported children and is not living in the same home as their spouse or partner, and—
the student’s spouse or partner is not enrolled in more than half of a full-time course:
the student’s spouse or partner is enrolled in more than half of a full-time course and—
is eligible for but does not receive, or is not eligible for, a grant

para.text: However, if the student’s personal income exceeds $284.70 for the week, the value of the basic grant payable to the student for the week is reduced by an amount equalling the excess.
Despite subclause (1), the basic grant is not payable for the week if the student’s combined income for the week is $1,185.75 or more.

history.history-note: Schedule 2 clause 2: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).
Schedule 2 Subpart 2: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).

prov.heading: Basic grant for week for single students

para.text: The following table sets out the value of a basic grant payable for a week to a single student:

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. Column 1 is headed Item. Column 2 is headed Circumstances. Column 3 is headed Value ($).

row.entry: The student has 1 or more supported children
The student is 24 or older, childless, and not living in a parental home
The student is 24 or older, childless, and living in a parental home
The student is under 24, childless, and not living in a parental home
The student is under 24, childless, and living in a parental home

para.text: However, subclause (1) is subject to subclauses (3) to (6).

If the student is under 24, childless, not living in a parental home, and the average gross weekly income of the student’s parents or parent exceeds $1,386.71, the value of the basic grant payable for a week to the student specified in item 4 of the table in subclause (1) is reduced by the amount calculated in accordance with the following formula:

eqn.eqn-line: (30.30303% × (the amount of item A up to a maximum of $259.51)) + (27.93296% × (any amount of item A exceeding $259.51))

para.text: where A is the average gross weekly income of the student’s parents or parent less $1,386.71.

If the student is under 24, childless, living in a parental home, and the average gross weekly income of the student’s parents or parent exceeds $1,386.71, the value of the basic grant payable for a week to the student specified in item 5 of the table in subclause (1) is reduced by the amount calculated in accordance with the following formula:

eqn.eqn-line: (30.30303% × (the amount of item A up to a maximum of $71.41)) + (27.93296% × (any amount of item A exceeding $71.41))

para.text: where A is the average gross weekly income of the student’s parents or parent less $1,386.71.
If the student’s personal income exceeds $284.70 for the week, the value of the basic grant payable for the week to the student is reduced by an amount equalling the excess.

If both subclauses (3) and (5) apply, or both subclauses (4) and (5) apply, the reduction described in subclause (3) or (4) (as the case requires) occurs before the reduction described in subclause (5).

history.history-note: Schedule 2 clause 3: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).
Schedule 2 Part 2: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).

prov.heading: Independent circumstances grant for week

para.text: The value of an independent circumstances grant payable for a week to a student is $378.64.
However, if the student’s personal income exceeds $284.70 for the week, the value of the independent circumstances grant for the week is reduced by an amount equalling the excess.

history.history-note: Schedule 2 clause 4: replaced, on 1 April 2026, by regulation 5 (and see regulation 4 for application) of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations 2026 (SL 2026/40).

schedule.heading: Maximum weekly value of accommodation benefit payable under regulation 24(1)

history.history-note: Schedule 3: replaced, on 1 April 2018, by regulation 4 of the Student Allowances (Budget 2017—Accommodation Benefit) Amendment Regulations 2017 (LI 2017/134).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 2 columns. Column 1 is headed Allowance and column 2 is headed Maximum weekly value.

row.entry: Accommodation benefit payable under regulation 24(1) (to students other than those to whom regulation 24A applies)

para.text: For any student, $60 per week

history.history-note: Schedule 3A: inserted, on 27 October 2016, by regulation 11 of the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2016 (LI 2016/217).

signature-block.sig.para: Marie Shroff,Clerk of the Executive Council.

promulgation.issue-authority: Issued under the authority of the Legislation Act 2019.
promulgation.gazette-date: Date of notification in Gazette: 24 September 1998.

made.heading: Order in Council
made.made.at: At Wellington this 25th day of August 2008
made.made.present: Present:His Excellency the Governor-General in Council

para.text: Pursuant to section 303 of the Education Act 1989, His Excellency the Governor-General, acting on the advice and with the consent of the Executive Council, makes the following regulations.

prov.heading: Title

para.text: These regulations are the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2008.

prov.heading: Commencement

para.text: Regulation 8 and Schedule 1 are deemed to have come into force on 1 April 2008.
Regulations 6(1) and 9 and Schedule 2 come into force on 1 October 2008.
Regulations 4, 5, 6(2), 7, 10, and 11 and Schedule 3 come into force on 1 January 2009.
The rest of these regulations come into force on the day after the date of their notification in the Gazette.

prov.heading: Transitional provision

para.text: Subclause (2) applies to any student who—
is partway through a course of study as at 1 January 2009; and
either—
is aged 24; or
is under the age of 24 and has a sibling aged 24 who is studying.
The allowances payable to a student who meets the criteria in subclause (1) must not be reduced as a result only of the amendments made by regulations 4, 5, 7, and 10.
Subclause (2) applies only until the first of the following occurs—
the student reaches the age of 25; or
the period of study in respect of which the allowance is paid ends; or
a change of circumstances makes the student eligible for a higher rate of entitlement under the principal regulations (as amended by these regulations).

signature-block.sig.officer: Rebecca Kitteridge,Clerk of the Executive Council.

promulgation.gazette-date: Date of notification in Gazette: 28 August 2008.

made.heading: Order in Council
made.made.at: At Wellington this 6th day of August 2012
made.made.present: Present:His Excellency the Governor-General in Council

para.text: Pursuant to section 303 of the Education Act 1989, His Excellency the Governor-General, acting on the advice and with the consent of the Executive Council, makes the following regulations.

prov.heading: Title

para.text: These regulations are the Student Allowances Amendment Regulations (No 2) 2012.

prov.heading: Commencement

para.text: These regulations, except as provided in subclause (2), come into force on 6 September 2012.
Regulations 8, 10, and 13 come into force on 1 January 2013.

prov.heading: Amendments consequential on revocation of regulations 21 and 22 and saving for approvals given under those regulations

para.text: Amendment(s) incorporated in the regulations.
Amendment(s) incorporated in the regulations.
Any approval given under regulation 21 or 22 (as in force immediately before their revocation by regulation 10 of these regulations) continues in effect according to its tenor.
Subclause (3) does not limit the Interpretation Act 1999.

signature-block.sig.para: Michael Webster,for Clerk of the Executive Council.

promulgation.gazette-date: Date of notification in Gazette: 9 August 2012.

para.text: This is a consolidation of the Student Allowances Regulations 1998 that incorporates the amendments made to the legislation so that it shows the law as at its stated date.

A consolidation is taken to correctly state, as at its stated date, the law enacted or made by the legislation consolidated and by the amendments. This presumption applies unless the contrary is shown.

Section 78 of the Legislation Act 2019 provides that this consolidation, published as an electronic version, is an official version. A printed version of legislation that is produced directly from this official electronic version is also an official version.

The Parliamentary Counsel Office makes editorial and format changes to consolidations using the powers under subpart 2 of Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019. See also PCO editorial conventions for consolidations.
